mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate profit visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE remains below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales signals expanding consumer demand, driving equity valuations higher through improved earnings expectations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where both HOUST and RSAFS show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive equity returns during economic expansion phases over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns in 60%+ of expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 330 in the CPI index prompts central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs signals robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods with PAYEMS growth above 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in quarterly GDP and monthly housing starts indicates expanding demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained economic momentum.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and CPI signal monetary tightening amid inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as risk assets face higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exceed thresholds.
